intro: Calyptranthes chytraculia, also known as the 'Wild Cinnamon Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and infections, due to its purported health benefits of antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calyptranthes chytraculia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been isolated and studied for their potential medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calyptranthes chytraculia is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues and respiratory infections.
